documentation_impact.rationale: ADR 0005 (timestamp-naming-conventions) encodes a three-level work path shape work/<day>/<task>/<artifact> for migrated and post-migration work directories. The pre-existing .cursor/hooks/enforce-policy-compliance.sh accepts only the legacy two-level shape work/<task>/policy-compliance.json via the regex ^work/[^/]+/policy-compliance\.json$. Without this fix, the migration-write commit and every subsequent commit landing artifacts under the new three-level shape would be rejected by the policy-compliance gate, locking the bootstrap. This fix extends the artifact discovery loop to accept both the legacy two-level and the ADR-0005 three-level shapes, while continuing to reject other depths.

deferred_items.rationale: Once the migration write completes and every existing work directory follows the three-level shape, the legacy two-level branch SHALL be removed in a follow-up slice. Removing it now would block the migration write commit itself, which still references legacy paths during the rename diff. Removal is queued behind the migration-write task.

policy_alignment.agents_md_alignment: Slice is mechanical infrastructure unblock for ADR 0005; no persona work or runtime semantics changed. The hook update preserves fail-closed behavior (other depths still rejected) and the validator's downstream invariants are untouched.
policy_alignment.constitution_alignment: Slice keeps the policy-compliance gate fail-closed, accepting only the two ratified shapes (legacy two-level and ADR-0005 three-level), and queues legacy-shape removal as a deterministic follow-up.
policy_alignment.prd_alignment: Slice satisfies the PRD §10 LocalUserAuthorizer working-agreement (humans gate at phase boundaries; commit-time gate stays enforced) while honoring the ADR 0005 path shape.
